qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Default-enabled auto-login route grants unauthenticated superuser access
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: This finding covers the default-enabled `/api/v1/auto_login` token-issuance path, not the advisory's unauthenticated `POST /api/v1/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow` path that accepts attacker-supplied flow data and drives it into the custom-component `exec()` chain.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final `qualifying_vuln` finding identifies the advisory's `build_public_tmp` root cause. The only qualifying report is a separate unauthenticated `auto_login` superuser bypass that may help satisfy advisory prerequisites under default settings, but it does not describe the advisory's attacker-supplied public-flow build data reaching unsandboxed `exec()`.
evidence: GHSA-vwmf-pq79-vjvx describes unauthenticated `POST /api/v1/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow` accepting attacker-controlled `data` and sending it through the graph-building path into `prepare_global_scope(...); exec(...)`.

The sole qualifying finding in this run targets `GET /api/v1/auto_login` in `src/backend/base/langflow/api/v1/login.py`, where default-enabled `AUTO_LOGIN` returns a superuser token and cookie; it does not involve `build_public_tmp`, public-flow graph substitution, or the custom-component execution chain.

The run does contain `public_flow_arbitrary_graph_execution`, which is advisory-adjacent and names the `build_public_tmp` endpoint, but that folder is `non_qualifying_vuln`; there is therefore no qualifying finding that matches the advisory-described vulnerable code/root cause.
